name: Crown Bank
description_short: Full-service commercial bank headquartered in Elizabeth, NJ with 14 branch locations across New Jersey, offering consumer and business banking services.
description_long: Crown Bank is a full-service commercial bank headquartered in Elizabeth, New Jersey, established as a community-focused financial institution. The bank operates fourteen branch offices across three distinct market areas of New Jersey, with twelve locations in the northern tier (Newark, Harrison, Kearny, Clifton, West New York, Union City, Elizabeth, Cranford, Dunellen, and Warren), one in South Jersey (Linwood), and one in Brick. The bank has received recognition from the Independent Community Bankers of America (ICBA) in both 2005 and 2010 for posting top 20 year-end earnings performances among ICBA member banks of its asset size. Crown Bank offers a comprehensive range of banking services to small businesses, professionals, and individuals. Consumer services include checking and savings accounts, check reordering, and mobile banking via their Crown Bank Mobile App. Business services encompass commercial accounts, business debit cards, escrow management, and cash management solutions including account transfers, wire funds, ACH payroll processing, and remote check deposit. The bank emphasizes personalized, relationship-oriented service and concentrates lending in commercial loans to local borrowers. Digital capabilities include Zelle money transfer integration, online bill pay with demo available, Crown at Home online banking platform, Business Direct for commercial clients, and Telebanc telephone banking. Crown Bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on personalized service and local community focus as an independent community bank. The bank maintains multiple physical branch locations for in-person service and combines traditional banking with modern digital services including mobile banking apps, online account management, and modern payment systems like Zelle. Their recognition by the ICBA for earnings performance demonstrates financial stability and operational success within the community banking sector. As a traditional community bank, Crown Bank serves customers seeking established banking relationships with local decision-making and multiple physical locations for transactions. However, prospective customers should note that detailed information about specific interest rates, fees, minimum account balances, and product terms is not available on the public website. The bank's lending focus on commercial loans to local borrowers may reflect more limited consumer lending products compared to larger national institutions.
services: Mobile banking app with check deposit
pros: 14 physical branch locations across New Jersey for in-person banking and customer service
Offers Zelle integration for fast money transfers between bank accounts
Mobile banking app with push notifications, bill pay, check deposit, and 24/7 account management
Business cash management services including ACH payroll processing, wire transfers, and remote check deposit
Recognized by Independent Community Bankers of America in 2005 and 2010 for top earnings performance
Telebanc telephone banking service for account access and transactions
FDIC insured deposits providing federal protection up to applicable limits
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to New Jersey only, with no online-only or national presence
Website lacks transparent information about interest rates, fees, minimum balances, and specific product terms
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or trust services beyond basic escrow management
Limited information about loan products, rates, and qualification criteria for consumers compared to business lending focus
best_for: Small business owners and professionals seeking local commercial banking relationships in New Jersey
Individuals and families in New Jersey preferring community bank service with multiple physical branch locations
Customers wanting modern digital banking features combined with traditional in-person service at local branches
diagnosis: Crown Bank is best for New Jersey-based consumers and small business owners seeking relationship banking with local decision-making and multiple physical branches combined with digital services. The main caveat is the lack of transparent pricing information on their website and limited product details, requiring customers to contact the bank directly for rates, fees, and specific terms.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
